code: source("Progs/data_import.R") options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  data_table <- data.frame(   name=c("data","codes"),   file=c("dirtydata.csv",          "codes.csv"),   folder=system.file("extdata", package="cctu"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )   read_data(data_table) read_data( data_table[2,])  # take an template meta table and add a new row with edited values. meta <- cctu::meta_table_example regress <- meta[1,] regress$title <- "Regression: Age predicted by Treatment" regress$number <- "1.2" meta <- rbind(meta, regress)  km <- meta[1,] km$title <- "Kaplan-Meier Plot" km$number <- "1.3" km$item <- "figure" meta <- rbind(meta, km) set_meta_table( meta)   write_table(data_table_summary(data_table),number = "9", clean_up = FALSE)   # Not strictly needed as the default is to apply these two functions inside of read_data(), but this # can be turned off with clean_names_option = FALSE , or remove_blank_rows_cols_option=FALSE data %<>% clean_names() %>% remove_blank_rows_cols() codes %<>% clean_names   for( x in unique(codes$var)){   code_df <-  subset(codes, var==x)   print(code_df)   data[,x] <- factor( data[,x], levels=code_df$code, labels=code_df$label) }  data$start_date <- as.POSIXct( data$start_date , format="%d/%m/%Y") data_name <- names(data) names(data)[match("subject_id", data_name)] <- "subjid"    #Create the population table  popn <- data[,"subjid", drop=FALSE] popn$safety <- TRUE popn$full <- popn$subjid<5  create_popn_envir("data",popn)  #tidy up rm(code_df, codes, data_name, x) .reserved <- ls()

what: What cttab() returns and how it renders
title: Use cttab in the Analysis
text: print method ’ve using small amount work behind scenes. cttab() returns long-format data.table (one row per (row_split, group level, variable, statistic) cell) carrying raw values, layout decisions made:  shape want need post-process numbers — filter rows, edit labels, bind two tables together (rbind.cttab() aware long format re-numbers Var_ID / Group_ID bound parts stay sections). also write_table() accepts given unformatted cttab. rendered shape — section banners inserted, headers stamped bold/bgcol/span tokens, stat rows indented — produced cttab_format(). print.cttab() write_table() call , normally don’t . Calling explicitly useful want inspect tweak rendered layout: result data.frame label column carrying per-row text row_style attribute carrying per-row ;-joined style tokens — vocabulary format_table() exposes (see next section). notes: cttab_format() honours nest attribute X_long, flipping nest = "split" ↔︎ "var" reflected rendered row order without second function call. cttab_format() handed table already formatted (matrix cttab data.frame stamped row_style attribute label column), returns input unchanged. makes safe write_table() print.cttab() call unconditionally. want tidy long-format frame downstream tools (rendering metadata), use .data.frame(X_long) — drops Group_ID, Group_Label, Var_ID, Stat_ID book-keeping columns keeps Variable, Statistic, Value, plus group / row_split columns.

what: Styling a custom table with format_table
title: Use cttab in the Analysis
text: Sometimes table want render isn’t cttab summary — maybe ’ve built small data.frame hand, run regression want drop tidy version report. write_table() knows render data.frame matrix styled row_style attribute, format_table() helper stamps attribute . styling vocabulary four tokens — bold, bgcol, span, indent — plus optional col token coloured text. pass one vector row indices, format_table() assembles per-row ;-joined string renderer consumes. machinery cttab_format() uses internally, look match cttab table. row appear section banner ends "bold;bgcol;span"; plain variable header "bold;span"; stat rows "indent"; everything else stays "". Token order assembled string fixed (bold;bgcol;span;indent;col), --range / NA row indices rejected column name error message. coloured fills text, pass bgcol_color / col_color. accept either six-digit hex string standard R colour name; named colours converted hex XSLT pipeline sees one form: col requires col_color set — supplying one without emits warning colour token dropped. want package default (light grey fill), just omit bgcol_color. stamped, hand table write_table() (print method) way cttab:
code: df <- data.frame(   label = c("Demographics",             "Age (years)", "Mean (SD)", "Median [Min, Max]",             "Sex",         "Female",    "Male"),   Treated = c("",          "",          "65.2 (8.1)", "66 [50, 82]",                            "",          "12 (40%)",   "18 (60%)"),   Control = c("",          "",          "63.9 (7.4)", "64 [49, 80]",                            "",          "15 (50%)",   "15 (50%)"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  df <- format_table(df,   bold   = c(1, 2, 5),  # banner + variable headers   bgcol  = 1,           # only the top "Demographics" banner gets a fill   span   = c(1, 2, 5),  # banner + variable headers span all columns   indent = c(3, 4, 6, 7) )  attr(df, "row_style") #> [1] "bold;bgcol;span" "bold;span"       "indent"          "indent"          #> [5] "bold;span"       "indent"          "indent" df <- format_table(df,   bgcol       = 1,   bgcol_color = "steelblue", # banner uses a custom fill   col         = c(3, 4),   col_color   = "#B22222"    # two stat rows in a deep red ) write_table(df)   # renders into Output/Core/table_<number>.xml

title: Summarise subject-level events by class and term — ae_summary
code: ae_summary(   data,   adsl,   id_var = cctu_opt("subjid_string"),   term_var,   class_var = NULL,   trt_var = NULL,   overall = TRUE,   grade_var = NULL,   grades = NULL,   grade_layout = c("vertical", "horizontal"),   grade_count = c("worst", "any"),   grade_report = c("all", "high"),   grade_high = 3L,   min_pct = 0,   class_sort = c("freq", "alpha"),   any_label = "Participants with any event" )

title: Summarise subject-level events by class and term — ae_summary
text: data Event-level dataset (one row per event). adsl Subject-level dataset providing denominators. id_var Subject ID column name (string). Must exist `data` `adsl`. term_var Preferred term column name `data` (string). class_var Class/SOC column name `data` (string), `NULL`. `NULL`, table flat: top "event" row followed term-level rows, class grouping. trt_var Optional treatment column `adsl` `data`. overall Add Total column `trt_var` given. grade_var Optional grade/severity column `data`. grades Grade levels report; defaults "grade" + observed. supplied, order taken ascending severity (worst last), also drives worst-grade counting. grade_layout `grade_var` given, "vertical" (default) nests grades rows beneath term: term row shows -grade count one indented sub-row per grade group gives subject count group. "horizontal" instead puts grade group column. Class/overall rows stay -grade. effect `grade_var` `NULL`. grade_count subject counted across grades given term (class). "worst" (default) counts subject , maximum grade, grade rows mutually exclusive sum -grade count. "" counts subject every grade experienced (rows may sum past -grade count). Worst-grade taken independently level. grade_report grade groups show. "" (default) reports grade. "high" reports single collapsed "Grade >=k" group (k = `grade_high`) alongside -grade row. Requires numeric grade labels (e.g. "Grade 3", "3"). grade_high Threshold k `grade_report = "high"`; default 3 gives "Grade >=3" group. min_pct Term-level frequency filter (percent); 0 disables. class_sort "freq" (default) "alpha". any_label Label top "event" row. Defaults "Subjects event"; pass e.g. "Patients AE" "Subjects medical history" match context.

title: Generate an table of descriptive statistics. — cttab
code: # Read data dt <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "pilotdata.csv", package="cctu")) dlu <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "pilotdata_dlu.csv", package="cctu")) clu <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "pilotdata_clu.csv", package="cctu"))  dt$subjid <- substr(dt$USUBJID, 8, 11)  # Apply variable attributes dt <- apply_macro_dict(dt, dlu, clu, clean_names = FALSE)  # Extract form data to be analysed df <- extract_form(dt, "PatientReg", vars_keep = c("subjid"))  ######################################################## #  Simple analysis no group and variable subset ###################################################### # Variable as a vector X <- cttab(x = c("AGE", "SEX", "BMIBL"),              data = df,              select = c("BMIBL" = "RACEN != 1"))   # Variable as a formula, equivalent to above X1 <- cttab(AGE + SEX + BMIBL ~ 1,             data = df,             select = c("BMIBL" = "RACEN != 1"))   ############################################# #  Analysis by group ############################################ # Variable as a vector X <- cttab(x = c("AGE", "SEX", "BMIBL"),              group = "ARM",              data = df,              select = c("BMIBL" = "RACEN != 1"))   ############################################ # Analysis by group and cycles ############################################  df <- extract_form(dt, "Lab", vars_keep = c("subjid", "ARM"))  X <- cttab(x = c("AST", "BILI", "ALT"),                   group = "ARM",                   data = df,                   row_split = "AVISIT",                   select = c("ALT" = "PERF == 1")) #> Dropped 142 observations with missing 'ARM' / 'AVISIT'.   ############################################ # Group variables ############################################  df <- extract_form(dt, "PatientReg", vars_keep = c("subjid")) base_lab <- extract_form(dt, "Lab", visit = "SCREENING", vars_keep = c("subjid"))  base_lab$ABNORMALT <- base_lab$ALT > 22.5 var_lab(base_lab$ABNORMALT) <- "ALT abnormal" base_lab$ABNORMAST <- base_lab$AST > 25.5 var_lab(base_lab$ABNORMAST) <- "AST abnormal"  df <- merge(df, base_lab, by = "subjid")  X <- cttab(x = list(c("AGE", "SEX", "BMIBL"),                       "Blood" = c("ALT", "AST"),                       "Patients with Abnormal" = c("ABNORMAST", "ABNORMALT")),           group = "ARM",           data = df,           select = c("BMIBL" = "RACEN != 1",                      "ALT" = "PERF == 1"))

title: Stamp row-style metadata on a table for `write_table()`. — format_table
text: x data.frame matrix. bold, bgcol, span, indent Integer vectors row indices (1-based) carry corresponding style. `NULL` (default) means rows style. Indices outside `[1, nrow(x)]` `NA` rejected error. bgcol_color color background rows `bgcol`. Accepts R color name (e.g. `"red"`) 6-digit hex string (e.g. `"FF0000"`). `NULL` (default) uses document default grey (`d3d3d3`). col Integer vector row indices whose text coloured. col_color color text rows `col`. format `bgcol_color`. Required `col` non-`NULL`.

title: Stamp row-style metadata on a table for `write_table()`. — format_table
text: `x`, unchanged shape, `attr(x, "row_style")` set   character vector length `nrow(x)`.

title: Stamp row-style metadata on a table for `write_table()`. — format_table
text: Tokens (renderer's vocabulary): `"bold"` (bold text), `"bgcol"` (grey background - typically banner row), `"span"` (first cell spans columns - typically header / banner row), `"indent"` (visual indent - typically stat row sitting header). row may carry combination, e.g. banner row appears `bold`, `bgcol`, `span`. Token order assembled string fixed: `bold;bgcol;span;indent;col`.

title: Stamp row-style metadata on a table for `write_table()`. — format_table
code: df <- data.frame(label = c("Group A", "n", "mean"), value = c("", "10", "3.4")) df <- format_table(df, bold = 1, bgcol = 1, span = 1, indent = 2:3) attr(df, "row_style") #> [1] "bold;bgcol;span" "indent"          "indent"          # [1] "bold;bgcol;span" "indent"          "indent"  # Custom background and text colors df2 <- format_table(df, bgcol = 1, bgcol_color = "steelblue", col = 2:3, col_color = "red") attr(df2, "row_style") #> [1] "bgcol:4682B4" "col:FF0000"   "col:FF0000"

code: # Read example data dt_a <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "test_A.csv", package="cctu"),                  colClasses = "character") dt_b <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "test_B.csv", package="cctu"),                  colClasses = "character")  # Read DLU and CLU dlu_a <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "test_A_DLU.csv", package="cctu")) dlu_b <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "test_B_DLU.csv", package="cctu")) clu_a <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "test_A_CLU.csv", package="cctu")) clu_b <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "test_B_CLU.csv", package="cctu"))  # Merge dataset with merge_data function res <- merge_data(datalist = list(dt_a, dt_b), dlulist = list(dlu_a, dlu_b),                   clulist = list(clu_a, clu_b)) dt <- res$data # Extract combined data dlu <- res$dlu # Extract combined DLU data clu <- res$clu # Extract combined CLU data  # Apply CLU and DLU files dt <- apply_macro_dict(dt, dlu = dlu, clu = clu, clean_names = FALSE)

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ##################################### # Below is a simple example ========# ##################################### # write_plot(plot_fn = plot, plot_args = list(x = iris[, 1], y = iris[, 2])) # This is equivalent drawing the following plot and save it plot(x = iris[, 1], y = iris[, 2])  # Below is user defined function plotting # One can use this method to draw a complicated plot new_plot <- function(x, y, h, v) {   par(pty = "s", cex = 0.7) # adjust plot style   plot(x, y)   abline(h = h, v = v, lty = 2) # add some lines } write_plot(   x = iris[, 1], y = iris[, 2], h = 2.5, v = 6.0,   plot_fn = new_plot )   #################################################### # To draw a KM-plot from survminer package ========# ####################################################  library("survival") library("survminer") fit <- survfit(Surv(time, status) ~ sex, data = lung) # Drawing survival curves p <- ggsurvplot(fit, data = lung) write_plot(p, plot_fn = survminer:::print.ggsurvplot) # The code above works because the p is a ggsurvplot object (check it with class(p)) # There's a printing function print.ggsurvplot to handle the printing of the KM-plot. # But this function is not exported by survminer, so we need to use three colons.  ##################################### # Draw a consort diagram ===========# #####################################  library(grid) # Might want to change some settings txt0 <- c("Study 1 (n=160)", "Study 2 (n=140)") txt1 <- "Population (n=300)" txt1_side <- "Excluded (n=15):\n\u2022 MRI not collected (n=15)"  # supports pipeline operator g <- add_box(txt = txt0) |>   add_box(txt = txt1) |>   add_side_box(txt = txt1_side) |>   add_box(txt = "Randomized (n=200)") # Since you can draw the plot g with plot(g), the ploting function is plot # The plotting function is \code{plot.consort}, so simple plot or plot.consort works write_plot(g, plot_fn = plot) # Or just write_plot(g) } # }

title: Function to write a text into xml format in the correct directory, and edit TableofTables — write_text
code: write_text(   x,   number = cctu_env$number,   clean_up = TRUE,   directory = file.path(cctu_opt("output"), "Core"),   verbose = options()$verbose )

title: Function to write a text into xml format in the correct directory, and edit TableofTables — write_text
text: x character string (vector strings) saved xml format. vector concatenated single text block. number number used suffix output filename, link meta_table. Default use value cctu_env package environment set within attach_pop. clean_up logical invoke clean_up function end. Defaults TRUE. directory save text within path current working directory. Defaults file.path(cctu_opt("output"), "Core"). verbose logical print information changes global environment external files. Defaults options()$verbose.

title: Function to write a text into xml format in the correct directory, and edit TableofTables — write_text
text: writes xml version input data file text_number.xml . Edits TableofTables object calling programe. return object.
